time in which real estate went up 10% in one year alone okay so that $100,000 at the end of the year would cost how much to buy $110,000 you paid $100,000 for it what is the profit in dollars $10,000 now let's let's learn a formula called yield how do you determine what rate of return you got on your money divide the profit by your cash investment let me show you something you paid cash for the property so you got a $10,000 profit divided by $100,000 cash investment what's the yield on your money 10% now I don't want you to do that what I want you to do is borrow $90,000 from the bank your tenant's going to make the payment for you you put $10,000 down as a down payment now pay attention to this when inflation and appreciation comes along does it know which properties have loans on them and which ones don't have loans on them so it goes up with the same amount doesn't it so you got the same house the same year the same appreciation but now let's look at it now you got $10,000 profit now to get the yield you divide it by how much cash you have in it which is how much this way 10 grand what's the yield on your money 100% you see wealthy people have learned you actually make more money when you use other people's money so even if you had money would you use it no that's hard for people who don't have any money to understand because they think if I had a lot of money I'd buy whatever I wanted to know if you have a lot of money it means you got it but knowing something and that knowledge tells you no you actually make more money using other people's money time out now thing that other guys did he said no no no it's not he said yes it is and see in most seminars it is an absolute no no to bring a tape recorder you never bring a tape recorder and my friend this was his pet peeve he says it is what the other guy said I got it right here on my tape recorder it was extremely embarrassing so by the way I don't care but when you're telling your story tell your story I'm going to give you some rules here of a testimonial a testimonial has three parts and you will see that most speakers butcher this part the three rules for a testimonial are very simple number one it needs to be a likable person number two they need to have a problem similar in nature to the one your audience has and number three they need to have overcome that problem with your product would you like an example of that? Just a minute though there was a lady I think her name was Lisa Lisa Lisa Lisa Lisa Lisa Lisa is about 50 some odd years old cute little woman about that tall she has short gray hair it's almost like mine you know I probably like her but when she smiles she's got dimples that go at each thick into her cheeks there and she came up there threw that big smile on me you know just cute as a button and she said Mr. Childers I love you know I want to do this so bad but just to tell you the truth I am scared to death to take on anything like this and I tried to talk to her for a little while and promised her that if she came to our training that I would remove that fear and she trusted me just enough she said okay I'll come but if I don't like it I want my money back I said that's a fair agreement she came to our training went through our learning exercises that are designed to remove fear and by the end of the training she wasn't talking about having fear anymore she was talking about the details of going out and making money so now that she's back in San Diego I think she's making like in the six digits over a hundred thousand dollars a year with these strategies time out is she a likeable person? What was her problem? Fear. Fear. Do you relate to this? Will your audience relate to fear? How did she overcome her fear? By coming to my training and going through these learning exercises that are designed to eliminate did she overcome her problem with my product? Now how many testimonials have you heard that didn't fit that model whatsoever? Almost all of them. Let me tell you about so and so got involved in real estate and they're making a pile of money right now. Now getting involved in real estate is not the answer to anybody's problem. See everybody then if you're teaching real estate, if you're teaching business, if you're teaching just a motivational type training, getting involved is not the objective here. Going through the training, absorbing the information in your product is the answer that it needs to have solved a problem here. Well, but all that aside let me tell you about my first story, my first property. I was 21 years old, single, high school dropout, I was sharing an apartment with, there were five of us in this apartment, you know we just kind of split the rent here and had a decent job and I wasn't really what we call dependable and you know being at work on time or even being at work wasn't how my priority list. So I had gone to an AMWI seminar. Anybody ever been to an AMWI meeting? Anybody hadn't been to an AMWI meeting? You know they got me all fired up. You know they told me about you know if you think positive you get positive results. If you think negative you get negative results and then they taught me another thing that I still remember today. I said if you don't like where you are financially, is that all of us? If you don't like where you are financially you got one or two choices. Either raise your income up to fit your wants and needs or lower your wants and needs down to fit your pitily income. Now I am making money by the hour. I don't have a lot of control over how much money I make so I only have one option now which is what? Lower my expenses. Now I had just bought a car, a fairly new car and I don't know how they, it seems like used car dealers can pull up magic in you know you can't borrow money anywhere else but you can borrow money to buy that car right? Now by the way here's a rule that wealthy people follow. Spend your money on things that go up in value versus things that go down in value. Now did I follow that rule? No I bought a car, is it going up in value? No it is going down in value. So now is that going to get me ahead financially? No. I'm just trying to tell you where I was. I was not doing the correct thing to become wealthy. I bought this car and then I found out that if I don't like where I am financially I can either make more money or lower my expenses. Now I don't know if you've ever been 21 years old single male with a bad driving record but the most expensive thing in your pay out goal is called what? Insurance. So I thought I'll lower my expenses. I dropped that insurance. Now you tell me two weeks to the day after I dropped that insurance tell me what happened. Boom I rear ended the tree. So now I got this car I got a fix and pay for that I can't even drive and I come home and by the way when you're sharing an apartment with other people when you don't pay your share of the rent they don't go through the proper eviction process. I came home and everything I owned dumped out in a hallway out there. So I have to have a place to live. Now I'm looking at the newspaper classified ads here. I'm not looking for a house for sale right? I'm not looking for a house for rent. I'm not looking for apartment for rent. I'm looking for a room for rent. You don't understand where I was financially. Maybe you've not ever been there but I was there. I'm looking for a room for rent and I have no car. I have my stuff in my hands. So I found a house kind of close. I mean an ad kind of close. I went over and knocked on the door and way said think positive. I knocked on the door and said I came to rent that room. The lady said something that if you remember the words will change your life. She said I'm sorry our situation has changed. I said well what do you mean? She said well my husband just got a job transfer so instead of renting these rooms out we're going to have to sell the house and go ahead and move to St. Louis. I said oh okay thank you. So I turned to leave and she said wait a minute. I see you're walking. You went to a lot of trouble to come look at this house. Maybe won't you just look through and say if you'd like to buy it. I said lady you don't understand. And she said something that was kind of interesting. She said I could give you a good deal. How many of you like the words good deal? And I said what do you mean by good deal? She said well look if you could make like a small down payment we would carry seller financing. Let you pay us by the month for our equity. And then you could just buy it and rent it out and whatever and I thought what do you mean by small down payment? She said how about $600. Is $600 a small down payment? Well folks this is 32 years ago, 37 years ago now right? Those of you who are old enough with $600 a small down payment 35, 37 years ago? No. And I had no $600. And she said well maybe I could give you some ideas where you could get that money. Okay. And she said maybe your employer would give you a cash advance. Now do I want to tell her that I just been fired? She said maybe you could borrow on your retirement plan. I'm not an executive. She said well maybe you could borrow on the equity of your car. Now are these ideas ideas I can use? Are the ideas that you might use sometime in your life? So when you hear ideas should you write them down or just make a decision? No they don't apply to me. So she said maybe your father would give it to you. You hadn't met my father. She said well maybe he'd loan it to you. And I thought a minute. Now of all the ideas she gave me only two of them had any possibilities and both of them required me to call my father. Now I can ask him to give me the money or loan me the money. Which should I try first? Give me the money is what you try first right? So I called him of his dad. The American dream homeownership dad. I know him only 21 years old but dad I've got an opportunity to own my own home. Would you give me $600 and boom he hung up on me. I called him back and said we got cut off. We got cut off just as I was about to ask you if you would loan me. Give me the $600. He said ha ha loan you the money. Well now that's different. He said tell you what I'll loan you the money but that's the last time I ever want to hear about this piece of real estate. You'll manage it. You'll maintain it. I don't ever want to hear about it again until you sell it. And when you sell it I won't have the profits of the deal. Now let's see. Was that a good deal for me? Because now I can buy a home that I couldn't buy before right? Was it a good deal for him? Well let's see. I moved into the garage and I rented a house out. Now I didn't say the garage apartment. I moved into the garage. You know I'm single. I need to be with my car. So I ran into the house out to a young couple and they had a couple of kids there. Four or five months later they came up there and said John do you ever think about selling this house? I said dude I never thought about buying this house. And they said you know we're all settled in the house. We love the school district. We'd like to make you an offer to buy the house. I looked at their offer and could not believe my eyes. Because based on that price I had just made enough money to pay off all my bills. My father way more than doubled and tripled his money. And I even put money into HIP National Bank. Now at that point I had learned a simple lesson. Take a simple strategy and do it again. And then what? Again. And again. Do you think my father was interested in doing another deal with me? Absolutely. Okay so do you think that when you know how to do certain things that make money are there always people out here that don't know how that will put up the money for you to do your stuff? Absolutely. And for the last 35, 40 years that's all the world I've been doing. Just the same thing. Over and over and over again. The same process. Time out. How's that story? Any subtle teaching in that? Okay now did I put up any money? Is that an important point to make in a story? I did not put up any money. Who'd I get? Someone else to put up a money. Now I got my father because in telling the story everybody relates to a family member would probably put it up. But he became a business partner not just someone who is going to give his son some money. Okay and by the way was it profitable for a business partner? Yes. Was it profitable for me? Yes. Because do I have any risk? Do I have any money whatsoever involved? So did I paint a picture of a financial transaction that an audience can see themselves doing over and over again? And did we also tell them enough of the details to let an audience see that partners are there over and over again? Because it is a win-win deal. Okay so when you tell your story make sure your story is one that has a message. Now some one said well what if our story is not like your story? I will venture to say that the older you get the story about how you did something many years changes as you tell it. Right? Because as I look back it wasn't nearly as funny when I was living it as it is now that I am telling. Okay so that becomes your story. Now at this point I want to show you something and I know that I am teaching a real estate training and teaching you one to do this year. But I have just told my story now watch what happens. Will everyone give you a good deal? No. What was the key to my getting a good deal? Can anybody remember? She needed to move? No. Ah I told you if you listen to these words it will make you a lot of money. Her situation had changed. That is the key. Now you tell me what kind of situations occur where you live that would make someone become motivated to give you a good deal? Any input? Divorce? Where do you live? California. It makes no difference does it where you live? Okay now when people want to get rid of each other they want to get rid of anything that ties each other together. Absolutely. What other kind of situations occur? Death in the family. Okay what else? Downsizing, illness, loss of job, job transfer. Rear ended in the tree with your car. Okay. Does the list go on and on by the way? It sure does. Okay now here is the question. If the answer is situations that change where do you find these situations? Where? Anywhere is not a really definitive answer. Courthouse? Where else? Newspaper? Where do you look in the newspaper? Learn from attorneys. Where else? Where do you look in the newspaper? Classified ads. Where do you look in the classified ads section? Where? Okay where else? Real estate section. Rental section? Where else? House for sale right? Do they have a for sale by owner section where you live? For sale by owner section. Now I am going to tell you I have been all over this country. I have never seen a for sale by owner section. You have to know how to look at an ad and tell if it is for sale by the owner or if it is some real estate agent. Do we have any real estate agents in the room? Anybody who has a license? Okay let me ask you a couple of questions. When you run an ad on a property you have listed, do you have to put the name of your company in the ad? Either that or some type of agent. In other words you either put the name of your company or something that tells the public that this is an agent listing here right? Okay so now what is the opposite of that? If there is no company name, no identifying them as being an agent then it is for sale by owner. So you have got to know how to look at the classified ad sections don't you? Again here is a little tip for you. Why don't you look at the for rent section? When you look at how many of you have ever known someone who wanted to sell a property and couldn't sell it? Have you ever known a situation like that? Okay what is an alternative to selling the property? Renting the property. In other words if you run an ad to rent a property a lot of those people they are saying I want to rent it but they really want to do what? Sell it. But people who call on the for rents are trying to rent it. I have been very successful in calling for rent ads and asking them if they would sell it to us. Okay time out. What I just demonstrated to you is this. Remember when I said that professional speakers will come up and say what happens when, what do you say when somebody asks you this, that, another? Now I have listened and watched speakers up there and I have had people in there say excuse me but these are not listed in the newspaper where we live. Excuse me, our courthouse will not release that information. Excuse me and they argue with the speaker. I want to notice something. I didn't tell you any of this stuff did I? Where did it come from? You told me. Isn't that interesting? Can you teach by asking questions? Okay all I said was the lady gave me a key piece of information. What was that piece of information? And you told me her situation changed. Wouldn't I say that? Okay now can you tell me, listen to this, can you tell me what situations might occur where you live? That's to nip this stuff in the bud of but John I'm from California, things don't work like that here. It never comes up in my presentation. Why? Because I didn't teach you anything. You were telling me. Because I said what situations occur where you live that might make someone motivated to give you a good deal and you just started telling me all kinds of information. I never said one thing. I repeated some divorce, lost a job, job transfer. I just said what you told me. Do you see why people can't pin you to the wall when you learn how to be a presenter by asking questions versus making statements? Now why did I go into the difference between a real estate agent's listing and a for sale by owner ad? What was the purpose of that? Knowledge? Tell me how that came into play. I gave an example of if you knew what a lot of people do not know what would it mean to you. See you throw in things where knowledge, people get their head shaking over there. That's interesting. I like knowing that. I'm not a big fan of that. I'm not a big fan of that. I'm not a big fan of that. I'm not a big fan of that. Because deep down they don't want to call a real estate agent. I know you know that already, right? Because most people think erroneously that they can get it cheaper if they go to the owner. Most people think erroneously that they'll do a better job if they do it themselves. Most people think erroneously that the price is going to be higher by going through a real estate agent. See you've got to give examples of how knowledge will change people's lives. Alright, time out. Let's take a 15 minute break and let's come back in here. How are you doing so far?
